a:r.a:t: Maturity assessment of foundational barriers across tools & data
Criteria
Observations
Leading Practice
Grade
System Integration
Compensation calculations rely on multiple disconnected systems
Data spans CRM, marketing, and finance platforms with limited integration
No single source of truth supports end-to-end compensation processing
Define a target-state source-to-report data flow across CRM, marketing, finance, and compensation processes
Clarify the role of each system in capturing, validating, processing, and reporting compensation data
Standardize data handoffs and control points between upstream and downstream systems
Use common identifiers and effective-dating rules across key records where possible
Reduce duplicate files, offline trackers, and email-based handoffs
Route non-standard scenarios through a governed workflow rather than unmanaged manual workarounds
Data Availability & Quality
Critical compensation data sits across multiple platforms
Manual reconciliations are needed to validate payouts
Limited controls and lineage increase downstream cleanup
Assign clear owners for critical compensation data elements and source feeds
Standardize business definitions for core fields used in compensation calculations and reporting
Set required fields, validation rules, and exception thresholds for key inputs
Introduce pre-payout data quality checks and reconciliation checkpoints
Maintain data lineage and version control for critical changes
Track recurring data issues and assign remediation ownership upstream
Reporting & Transparency
Reporting provides limited visibility into calculation logic and performance drivers
Leadership has limited ability to monitor compensation performance consistently
Low transparency can reduce seller trust in payouts
Standardize role-based reporting views for sellers, managers, admins, and leadership
Show payout drivers, adjustments, exceptions, and calculation status more clearly
Use common KPI definitions across operational and management reporting
Establish a consistent refresh cadence and reporting cut-off rules
Improve audit visibility into approvals, changes, and prior-period adjustments
Provide a clear path to investigate payout questions using shared reporting views
Automation of Calculations
Current Calidus and future Varicent tools support calculations for sales but not other teams
Sales Ops and Finance perform manual reconciliations across systems
Core compensation administration remains highly manual
Prioritize automation for stable, repeatable crediting and payout rules
Standardize calculation logic before automating to avoid hard-coding exceptions
Automate routine workflow steps such as intake, approval routing, and status tracking where practical
Reserve manual intervention for governed exceptions only
Embed control checks and reconciliation steps into the process flow
Monitor exception volumes to identify the next best automation opportunities
Compensation Governance Assessment
Governance Structure
Planning & Process
Tools & Data
Limited Issues / Minor Opportunities for Improvement
Moderate Issues / Clear Opportunity for Improvement
Significant Issues Discovered / Action Required to Address Gaps
